definitions: In spite of any indications or expectations to the contrary; nevertheless.
Used to introduce a statement that provides a reason or explanation for something previously said or implied.
Used to emphasize that something is true or has happened, often despite earlier doubts or plans.
examples: It was a difficult task, but we finished it after all.
I'm not surprised he won; after all, he's the best player.
She decided to go to the party after all.
